summary: Iteration 2 regression after backend + frontend refactor. Backend: 14/14 pytest cases pass (REST + WS protocol). Frontend: login → create room → connect WS → upload MP3 → display track → toggle play/pause all work; participants-count StrictMode bug from iteration 1 is RESOLVED (badge shows '1' for single user thanks to the wsRef readyState guard in useRoomSocket.connect()). Chat sending via Enter key works correctly (verified with text 'Второе' appearing in scroll). Click on chat-send-button still does not fire its onClick handler in the preview env at 1920x1080 — the platform-injected 'Made with Emergent' badge in the bottom-right corner sits on top of the send button and intercepts pointer events even with Playwright force=True (force only bypasses actionability checks, the click still dispatches at the page coordinate where the badge is topmost). This is a preview-env artefact and does NOT exist in production builds (the badge comes from @emergentbase devDependency).

ui_bugs.component: ChatPanel send button overlap (preview env only)
ui_bugs.issue: The platform-injected 'Made with Emergent' badge in the bottom-right corner overlaps the chat-send-button at 1920x1080. Real users can still send via Enter key (verified working). Send button is at right edge ~1870px; badge sits over it. Production builds do not include this badge so no end-user impact. Optional mitigation: add bottom padding/margin to the chat input row, or move the send button further from the right edge in the room layout so the badge doesn't overlap.

action_items: (Optional, cosmetic only) Slightly reduce chat send-button position from the right edge of the viewport, or add a bit of bottom-right padding to the room layout, so the platform-injected 'Made with Emergent' badge doesn't visually overlap the send button in the preview environment. Not required for production.
No behavioral changes required — all features verified working.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~545 lines, cleanly factored into Room class, helpers, upload helpers, REST endpoints, and per-message WS handlers. Good readability win over iteration 1.
secrets.choice is correctly used for both room codes and file IDs — replaces random.choice from iteration 1, addresses the predictability concern.

_read_upload_to_disk streams the upload chunk-by-chunk and enforces MAX_FILE_SIZE during read (no more buffer-in-memory). One small caveat: if a write raises mid-stream the partial file is not deleted — wrapping in try/except + unlink(missing_ok=True) would harden it.
websocket_endpoint still silently ignores messages with unknown 'type' — could send {type: 'error', message: 'unknown message type'} back to the client for easier debugging.

useRoomSocket.connect() correctly guards against re-entry via wsRef.current.readyState check (CONNECTING/OPEN → return). This fixes the React.StrictMode double-mount issue from iteration 1 — confirmed by participants badge showing 1.
useRoomSocket reconnect timer uses a fixed 2s. Consider exponential backoff if reconnects fail repeatedly (low priority).

Room.jsx is down to ~420 lines and uses useCallback for syncWithServer/applyState — the deps look correct (applyState depends on syncWithServer; syncWithServer has []). No stale-closure risks observed in testing.

ChatPanel message key `${m.ts}-${m.name}` is stable; if two messages from the same user share an identical ts (sub-ms collision possible under load) React would warn — extremely unlikely in practice but adding an index fallback would be bulletproof.
Frontend: console.warn statements in catch blocks of useRoomSocket and Room.jsx are good and improve debuggability.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all features work; only preview-env badge overlap blocks programmatic click on send button; Enter-key send works)

test_credentials: No auth required. Any name min 2 chars; e.g. 'Алексей', 'Тестер'. Room codes are 6 digits from POST /api/rooms.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests create rooms and synthetic MP3s on the fly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_syncplay.py covers all REST (root, rooms CRUD, upload with mp3-only + 20MB cap + ID3 metadata + GET-after-POST persistence, file-serve path-traversal block) and WebSocket protocol (join/init, missing-room error, two-client sync of play/pause/seek/volume/chat/ping, chat history on rejoin). All 14 pass. Frontend regression confirmed: StrictMode dup-participants RESOLVED (participants tab shows 1 for solo). Known cosmetic: 'Made with Emergent' badge overlaps chat-send-button in preview at 1920x1080; doesn't affect prod or Enter-key sending. WebSocket onerror logs a benign 'closed before established' warning during StrictMode cleanup — expected and harmless.
rca of the issue: Chat-send-button click in Playwright at 1920x1080 preview env: The /node_modules/@emergentbase visual-edits badge is appended to <body> at z-index above the chat input row and positioned at bottom-right (x≈1740-1900, y≈1015-1075). Playwright's force=True bypasses element-actionability checks but still dispatches the click at the page coordinate of the target element — and at that coordinate the badge is topmost in the stacking context, so the click is consumed by the badge instead of the cyan send button (icon-sized 40x40 at right edge ~1870px). End users on production deployments do not have this badge so the button is fully clickable. Reproduction-only-in-preview confirmed by: (1) Enter-key send works end-to-end, and (2) backend pytest two-client WS test shows chat broadcast works. Mitigation if desired: bump the chat input row's right margin/padding by ~80px in ChatPanel.jsx so the send button sits clear of the badge zone, OR rely on Enter-to-send (already works).
